---
title: "GG_Sidedness_3var"
author: "D. Grespin"
date: "2026-01-14"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

## Load Libraries

```{r load-libraries}

library(R.matlab)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(ggbreak)
library(dplyr)
library(effectsize)

```

## Load and arrange Matlab data

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r load-variables}

# read MATLAB .mat file into R (make sure to copy full path or place in your working directory)
distVintStruct1 <- readMat('/Users/drew.grespin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofDenver/cav1_project/dataMats/track_dataMats/track4_forR_Mar25.mat')
distVintStruct2 <- readMat('/Users/drew.grespin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofDenver/cav1_project/dataMats/track_dataMats/track8_forR_Mar25.mat')
distVintStruct3 <- readMat('/Users/drew.grespin/Library/CloudStorage/OneDrive-UniversityofDenver/cav1_project/dataMats/track_dataMats/track24_forR_Mar25.mat')

# Load raw data
distVintRaw1 <- distVintStruct1$track4.Mar25            # pull proper variable from MATLAB structure
dfRaw1       <- data.frame(
                           Normalized_Distance  = distVintRaw1[[5]][,1],
                           Normalized_Intensity = distVintRaw1[[5]][,2]
                          )

distVintRaw2 <- distVintStruct2$track8.Mar25                   # pull proper variable from MATLAB structure
dfRaw2       <- data.frame(
                           Normalized_Distance  = distVintRaw2[[5]][,1],
                           Normalized_Intensity = distVintRaw2[[5]][,2]
                          )

distVintRaw3 <- distVintStruct3$track24.Mar25                   # pull proper variable from MATLAB structure
dfRaw3       <- data.frame(
                           Normalized_Distance  = distVintRaw3[[5]][,1],
                           Normalized_Intensity = distVintRaw3[[5]][,2]
                          )

```

## Isolate desired data segments for analysis

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r isolate-segments}

# Split df1 into high and low

dfRaw1_rear  <- dfRaw1[dfRaw1$Normalized_Distance <= 0.1, ]
dfRaw1_front <- dfRaw1[dfRaw1$Normalized_Distance >  0.1, ]

# Split df2 into high and low
dfRaw2_rear   <- dfRaw2[dfRaw2$Normalized_Distance <= 0.1, ]
dfRaw2_front  <- dfRaw2[dfRaw2$Normalized_Distance >  0.1, ]

# Split df3 into high and low
dfRaw3_rear   <- dfRaw3[dfRaw3$Normalized_Distance <= 0.1, ]
dfRaw3_front  <- dfRaw3[dfRaw3$Normalized_Distance >  0.1, ]

nrow(dfRaw1_rear)
nrow(dfRaw1_front)
nrow(dfRaw2_rear)
nrow(dfRaw2_front)
nrow(dfRaw3_rear)
nrow(dfRaw3_front)

```

## Set up data frame for plotting

```{r df-setup}

dfPlot <- data.frame(
                     Intensity = c(
                       dfRaw1_rear$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw1_front$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw2_rear$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw2_front$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw3_rear$Normalized_Intensity,
                       dfRaw3_front$Normalized_Intensity
                     ),
                     Protein = factor(c(
                       rep("4",  length(dfRaw1_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("4",  length(dfRaw1_front$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("8",  length(dfRaw2_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("8",  length(dfRaw2_front$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("24", length(dfRaw3_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("24", length(dfRaw3_front$Normalized_Intensity))
                     ), levels = c("4", "8", "24")),
                     
                     Region = factor(c(
                       rep("Rear",  length(dfRaw1_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Front", length(dfRaw1_front$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Rear",  length(dfRaw2_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Front", length(dfRaw2_front$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Rear",  length(dfRaw3_rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
                       rep("Front", length(dfRaw3_front$Normalized_Intensity))
                     ), levels = c("Rear", "Front"))
                    )

xpos_map <- c(
              "4 Rear"   = 1.0,
              "4 Front"  = 1.4,
              "8 Rear"   = 1.9,
              "8 Front"  = 2.3,
              "24 Rear"  = 2.8,
              "24 Front" = 3.2
             )

dfPlot$x_pos <- xpos_map[paste(dfPlot$Protein, dfPlot$Region)]

```

## Compute stats for custom box & whiskers (IQR)

```{r iqr-compute}

box_stats <- dfPlot %>%
  group_by(Region, x_pos) %>%
  summarise(
            lower   = quantile(Intensity, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
            median  = quantile(Intensity, 0.50, na.rm = TRUE),
            upper   = quantile(Intensity, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = "drop"
           )

```

## Compute stats for confidence interval & Cohen's d

```{r stat-compute}

rear_int4 <- dfPlot %>%
                 filter(Protein == "4", Region == "Rear") %>%
                 pull(Intensity)

front_int4 <- dfPlot %>%
                 filter(Protein == "4", Region == "Front") %>%
                 pull(Intensity)

rear_int8 <- dfPlot %>%
                 filter(Protein == "8", Region == "Rear") %>%
                 pull(Intensity)

front_int8 <- dfPlot %>%
                 filter(Protein == "8", Region == "Front") %>%
                 pull(Intensity)

rear_int24 <- dfPlot %>%
                 filter(Protein == "24", Region == "Rear") %>%
                 pull(Intensity)

front_int24 <- dfPlot %>%
                 filter(Protein == "24", Region == "Front") %>%
                 pull(Intensity)

cohens_d(rear_int4,  front_int4)
cohens_d(rear_int8,  front_int8)
cohens_d(rear_int24, front_int24)
cohens_d(rear_int4,  rear_int8)
cohens_d(rear_int4,  rear_int24)
cohens_d(rear_int8,  rear_int24)

mean(rear_int4)
mean(front_int4)
mean(rear_int4) - mean(front_int4)

t.test(rear_int4, front_int4)$conf.int

mean(rear_int8)
mean(front_int8)
mean(rear_int8) - mean(front_int8)

t.test(rear_int8, front_int8)$conf.int

mean(rear_int24)
mean(front_int24)
mean(rear_int24) - mean(front_int24)

t.test(rear_int24, front_int24)$conf.int

```

## Sample (for large datasets) and compute statistical significance between groups

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r signif-test}

# Rear comparison 4 to 8 hours
pval_rear_4to8 <- t.test(
                         dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "4" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                         dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "8" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"]
                        )$p.value

# Front comparison 4 to 8 hours
pval_front_4to8 <- t.test(
                          dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "4" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"],
                          dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "8" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                         )$p.value

# Rear comparison 4 to 24 hours
pval_rear_4to24 <- t.test(
                          dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "4" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                          dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "24" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"]
                         )$p.value

# Front comparison 4 to 24 hours
pval_front_4to24 <- t.test(
                           dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "4" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"],
                           dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "24" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                          )$p.value

# Rear comparison 8 to 24 hours
pval_rear_8to24 <- t.test(
                          dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "8" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                          dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "24" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"]
                         )$p.value
 
# Front comparison 8 to 24 hours
pval_front_8to24 <- t.test(
                           dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "8" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"],
                           dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "24" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                          )$p.value

# 4 hours front to rear comparison
pval_4 <- t.test(
                 dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "4" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                 dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "4" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                )$p.value

# 8 hours front to rear comparison
pval_8 <- t.test(
                 dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "8" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                 dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "8" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                )$p.value

# 24 hours front to rear comparison
pval_24 <- t.test(
                  dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "24" & dfPlot$Region == "Rear"],
                  dfPlot$Intensity[dfPlot$Protein == "24" & dfPlot$Region == "Front"]
                 )$p.value

## =========================
## Significance bars (ALL)
## =========================

# Helper: p-value -> significance label
p_to_label <- function(p) {
  ifelse(p < 0.001, "***",
         ifelse(p < 0.01, "**",
                ifelse(p < 0.05, "*", "ns")))
}

# Build table of ALL comparisons you ran
sig_tests <- data.frame(
  x_start = c(
    xpos_map["4 Rear"],  xpos_map["4 Front"],   # 4 -> 8
    xpos_map["4 Rear"],  xpos_map["4 Front"],   # 4 -> 24
    xpos_map["8 Rear"],  xpos_map["8 Front"],   # 8 -> 24
    xpos_map["4 Rear"],  xpos_map["8 Rear"],    # Rear vs Front
    xpos_map["24 Rear"]
  ),
  x_end = c(
    xpos_map["8 Rear"],  xpos_map["8 Front"],
    xpos_map["24 Rear"], xpos_map["24 Front"],
    xpos_map["24 Rear"], xpos_map["24 Front"],
    xpos_map["4 Front"], xpos_map["8 Front"],
    xpos_map["24 Front"]
  ),
  pval = c(
    pval_rear_4to8,  pval_front_4to8,
    pval_rear_4to24, pval_front_4to24,
    pval_rear_8to24, pval_front_8to24,
    pval_4, pval_8, pval_24
  )
)

y_start      <- 1.25
y_step       <- 0.19
y_within_grp <- 1.06   # fixed y for Rear vs Front comparisons

sig_lines <- sig_tests %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # identify Rear vs Front within the same protein
    within_group =
      (x_start == xpos_map["4 Rear"]  & x_end == xpos_map["4 Front"])  |
      (x_start == xpos_map["8 Rear"]  & x_end == xpos_map["8 Front"])  |
      (x_start == xpos_map["24 Rear"] & x_end == xpos_map["24 Front"])
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    # assign y positions
    y = ifelse(
      within_group,
      y_within_grp,
      y_start + y_step * (dplyr::row_number() - 1 - cumsum(within_group))
    ),
    label = p_to_label(pval)
  )

```

## Preset theme elements

```{r theme-setup}

# Define colors
region_colors <- c("Rear" = "red", "Front" = "green")

# Define line parameters
median_length   <- 0.3
quartile_length <- 0.2

median_size   <- 4
quartile_size <- 3

# Define theme
plot_theme <- theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title         = element_text(size = 20, face = 'bold', hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'),
    axis.title         = element_text(size = 16, face = 'bold', hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial'),
    axis.text.y        = element_text(size = 72, hjust = 0.5, family = 'Arial', face = 'bold'),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(linewidth = 4, color = 'gray40'),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "none"
  )

```

## Setup plot features

```{r plot-setup}

boxplot <- ggplot(dfPlot, aes(x = x_pos, y = Intensity)) +
           # Raw points
           geom_jitter(
                       width = 0.14,
                       alpha = 0.05,
                       size  = 0.5,
                       color = "black"
           ) +
           # Vertical IQR line
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(x = x_pos, xend = x_pos, y = lower, yend = upper, color = Region),
                        size = quartile_size
                       ) +
           # Q1 and Q3 horizontal lines
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(
                            x     = x_pos - quartile_length / 2,
                            xend  = x_pos + quartile_length / 2,
                            y     = lower,
                            yend  = lower,
                            color = Region
                           ),
                           size = quartile_size
                       ) +
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(
                            x     = x_pos - quartile_length / 2,
                            xend  = x_pos + quartile_length / 2,
                            y     = upper,
                            yend  = upper,
                            color = Region
                           ),
                           size = quartile_size
                       ) +
           # Median line
           geom_segment(
                        data = box_stats,
                        aes(
                            x     = x_pos - median_length / 2,
                            xend  = x_pos + median_length / 2,
                            y     = median,
                            yend  = median,
                            color = Region
                           ),
                        size = median_size
                       ) +
           #X/Y scales
           scale_x_continuous(
                              breaks = c(1.0, 1.4, 2.2, 2.6, 3.4, 3.8),
                              labels = c("", "", "", "", "", ""),
                              expand = c(0, 0)
                             ) +
           scale_y_continuous(
                              limits = c(-.1, 2.45),
                              breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.25),
                              expand = c(0, 0)
                             ) +
           # Color of IQR lines
           scale_color_manual(
                              values = c(region_colors)  # combined for points and segments
                             ) +
           # Remove axis labels
           labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
           # add plot set up elements
           plot_theme

```

## Add significance to plot

```{r signif-plot}

boxplot <- boxplot +
           geom_segment(
                        data = sig_lines,
                        aes(x = x_start,
                            xend = x_end,
                            y = y,
                            yend = y),
                        color = "black",
                        size = 6
                       ) +
           geom_text(
                       data = sig_lines,
                              aes(
                                  x = (x_start + x_end) / 2,
                                  y = y + ifelse(label == "ns", 0.17, 0.01),
                                  label = label
                                 ),
                              size = 50
                    )

```

## Plot Output

```{r print-plot, fig.width = 22, fig.height = 11}

boxplot

```

```{r ratio-finder}

ratio_df <- dfPlot %>%
  group_by(Protein, Region) %>%
  summarise(mean_int = mean(Intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Region, values_from = mean_int) %>%
  mutate(ratio = Rear / Front)
  
## Rear/Front ratio plot

ratio_plot <- ggplot(ratio_df, aes(x = Protein, y = ratio)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, fill = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 32) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_blank(),   # removes "5 10 15 20 10A"
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()   # removes tick marks
       ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
                              limits = c(0, 1.6),
                              breaks = seq(0, 1.5, 0.5),
                              expand = c(0, 0)
                             )
  
```

```{r ratio-plot, fig.width=3.5, fig.height=2.5}
ratio_plot

```

```{r big-ratio-finder}

# =========================
# BUILD BIG RATIO DATASET
# =========================

split_df <- function(df) {
  list(
    rear  = df[df$Normalized_Distance <= 0.1, ],
    front = df[df$Normalized_Distance >  0.1, ]
  )
}

make_ratio_df <- function(df1, df2, df3, idx, prefix = "") {
  
  # extract correct column index
  d1 <- data.frame(
    Normalized_Distance  = df1[[idx]][,1],
    Normalized_Intensity = df1[[idx]][,2]
  )
  d2 <- data.frame(
    Normalized_Distance  = df2[[idx]][,1],
    Normalized_Intensity = df2[[idx]][,2]
  )
  d3 <- data.frame(
    Normalized_Distance  = df3[[idx]][,1],
    Normalized_Intensity = df3[[idx]][,2]
  )
  
  s1 <- split_df(d1)
  s2 <- split_df(d2)
  s3 <- split_df(d3)
  
  df <- data.frame(
    Intensity = c(
      s1$rear$Normalized_Intensity,  s1$front$Normalized_Intensity,
      s2$rear$Normalized_Intensity,  s2$front$Normalized_Intensity,
      s3$rear$Normalized_Intensity,  s3$front$Normalized_Intensity
    ),
    Protein = factor(c(
      rep("4",  length(s1$rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("4",  length(s1$front$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("8",  length(s2$rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("8",  length(s2$front$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("24", length(s3$rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("24", length(s3$front$Normalized_Intensity))
    )),
    Region = factor(c(
      rep("Rear",  length(s1$rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("Front", length(s1$front$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("Rear",  length(s2$rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("Front", length(s2$front$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("Rear",  length(s3$rear$Normalized_Intensity)),
      rep("Front", length(s3$front$Normalized_Intensity))
    ))
  )
  
  df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Protein, Region) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(mean_int = mean(Intensity, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Region, values_from = mean_int) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      ratio = Rear / Front,
      Protein = paste0(prefix, Protein)
    )
}

# ORIGINAL (index 5)
ratio_main <- make_ratio_df(distVintRaw1, distVintRaw2, distVintRaw3, 5, "")

# ALT (index 1)
ratio_alt  <- make_ratio_df(distVintRaw1, distVintRaw2, distVintRaw3, 1, "Alt_")

# COMBINE
ratio_df_big <- rbind(ratio_alt, ratio_main)
ratio_df_big$Protein <- factor(
  ratio_df_big$Protein,
  levels = c("Alt_4", "Alt_8", "Alt_24", "4", "8", "24")
)

# =========================
# BIG RATIO PLOT
# =========================

ratio_plot <- ggplot(ratio_df_big, aes(x = Protein, y = ratio)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5, fill = "grey40") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 32) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = ""
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x  = element_blank(),   # removes "5 10 15 20 10A"
    axis.ticks.x = element_blank()   # removes tick marks
       ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
                              limits = c(0, 3.1),
                              breaks = seq(0, 3, 0.5),
                              expand = c(0, 0)
                             )
  
  
```

```{r big-ratio-plot, fig.width=4, fig.height=3}
ratio_plot

```
